January 2026
This performance metrics document was prepared for the January 2026 meeting of the Bridges and Tunnels Committee.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: MTA Bridges and Tunnels
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Bridges and Tunnels is comprised of over 1,000 employees and operates seven bridges and two tunnels in New York City, handling over 330 million vehicle crossings every year in addition to managing the Congestion Relief Zone.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Bridges and Tunnels is comprised of over 1,000 employees and operates seven bridges and two tunnels in New York City, handling over 330 million vehicle crossings every year in addition to managing the Congestion Relief Zone.

tool_args.label: MTA Construction & Development
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Construction & Development is in the early construction stages of a major project to rehabilitate the Brooklyn interchange of the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge (VNB).

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Construction & Development is in the early construction stages of a major project to rehabilitate the Brooklyn interchange of the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge (VNB).

tool_args.label: Rehabilitating Brooklyn Ramps at Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Construction & Development is in the early construction stages of a major project to rehabilitate the Brooklyn interchange of the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge (VNB). The four-year, $435 million design-build project, the largest ever undertaken by MTA Bridges and Tunnels, was awarded in August 2025 to VNB Construction Company.

payload.project_name: Rehabilitating Brooklyn Ramps at Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge
payload.description: Rehabilitation of the Brooklyn interchange of the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge, including elevated roadways connecting to Belt Parkway, eastbound Gowanus Expressway, and 92nd Street. Includes concrete deck and barrier replacement, seismic improvements, pier repairs, and reconstruction of left-lane exit ramps to right-lane exits.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Construction & Development is in the early construction stages of a major project to rehabilitate the Brooklyn interchange of the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge (VNB). The four-year, $435 million design-build project, the largest ever undertaken by MTA Bridges and Tunnels, was awarded in August 2025 to VNB Construction Company.
The project will rehabilitate the elevated roadways connecting the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge to the Belt Parkway, the eastbound Gowanus Expressway and 92nd Street in Brooklyn.
The project is expected to be completed July 2029.

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote must be 280 characters or fewer
evidence_refs[1].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: Rehabilitating Brooklyn Ramps at Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Construction & Development is in the early construction stages of a major project to rehabilitate the Brooklyn interchange of the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge (VNB). The four-year, $435 million design-build project, the largest ever undertaken by MTA Bridges and Tunnels, was awarded in August 2025 to VNB Construction Company.

payload.project_name: Rehabilitating Brooklyn Ramps at Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge
payload.description: Rehabilitation of the Brooklyn interchange of the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge, including elevated roadways connecting to Belt Parkway, eastbound Gowanus Expressway, and 92nd Street. Includes concrete deck and barrier replacement, seismic improvements, pier repairs, and reconstruction of left-lane exit ramps to right-lane exits.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Construction & Development is in the early construction stages of a major project to rehabilitate the Brooklyn interchange of the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge (VNB).
The project will rehabilitate the elevated roadways connecting the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge to the Belt Parkway, the eastbound Gowanus

Expressway and 92nd Street in Brooklyn. Rehabilitation work includes replacement of the concrete deck and barriers, including all associated utilities and appurtenances, implementation of seismic improvements, and repairs to the original supporting piers.
The project is expected to be completed July 2029.
